items.title: Mistral shipped its first robotics model
items.oneliner: Mistral's Robostral Navigate brings plain-language robot navigation to factories, pushing foundation models from chat into physical operations.

items.title: GPT-5.6 beat Claude in production
items.oneliner: Ploy says GPT-5.6 made its agent 2.2x faster and 27% cheaper, which is the metric buyers actually care about.

items.title: Microsoft is cutting OpenAI dependence
items.oneliner: Microsoft is routing some Copilot prompts to internal models, signaling margin pressure is finally reshaping model sourcing.

items.title: Linux carried a 15-year root bug
items.oneliner: GhostLock let unprivileged users gain root and escape containers for 15 years, which should terrify every platform team.

items.title: Grok CLI reportedly uploads too much
items.oneliner: A wire-level analysis says Grok Build CLI uploads whole repos unredacted, making default trust in coding agents look reckless.

items.title: Browser math became an OS fingerprint
items.oneliner: Tiny Math.tanh differences now reveal operating systems, giving anti-bot vendors a weirdly effective new fingerprinting signal.

items.title: A police drone leak exposed surveillance
items.oneliner: Leaked SFPD drone footage showed live urban tracking in practice, turning abstract surveillance fears into operational reality.

items.title: Memory shortages could hit AI harder
items.oneliner: SK Hynix says the worst memory shortage arrives in 2027, a reminder that AI scaling still bottlenecks on hardware.

items.title: Layer 7 firewalls moved into kernels
items.oneliner: An eBPF-based L7 firewall now makes application-layer decisions in-kernel, which could rewrite latency expectations for traffic enforcement.

items.title: crates.io added better supply-chain visibility
items.oneliner: crates.io now lets you browse published source and flags unmaintained crates, making dependency audits less of a blindfolded exercise.
